preamble: The de-retroflexion of the voiceless retroflex stop [ʈ] into the voiceless alveolar affricate [ts] is a common phonetic shift in several Southern Chinese dialects, where retroflexes merge with dentals.

examples.note: Southern Chinese speakers often replace retroflex consonants with their alveolar counterparts.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
